summary: This notebook implements a gradient boosting ensemble for the MABe mouse behavior detection competition, focusing on transforming raw 2D pose tracking coordinates into rich, FPS-scaled time-series features for single mice and mouse pairs. It loads pre-trained CatBoost and XGBoost models, optimizes their per-action ensemble weights and probability thresholds using Optuna on out-of-fold predictions, and applies rigorous post-processing to ensure valid submission formats. Readers should learn how to derive domain-specific kinematic features from pose data, how to effectively combine multiple gradient boosting models via Optuna, and why careful frame-level post-processing is critical for sparse action recognition tasks.
competition_flow: Loads CSV metadata and parquet tracking/annotation files, extracts FPS-scaled time-series features for single mice and pairs, loads pre-trained CatBoost/XGBoost models, optimizes ensemble weights and thresholds via Optuna, and generates a robustified submission CSV.
data_reading: pd.read_parquet for tracking and annotation files in /kaggle/input/MABe-mouse-behavior-detection/{train,test}_tracking/ and train_annotation/
data_processing: Scales rolling windows to physical time using video FPS
Handles sparse labels and overlapping predictions per agent/target pair
Extends predictions to video end and fills empty videos with fallback actions
features_engineering: Curvature features: velocity, acceleration, cross product, curvature, turn rate with FPS-scaled rolling windows
Multiscale speed features: mean, std, and ratio over multiple window sizes
State features: speed discretization into bins with rolling state probabilities and transition counts
Long-range features: rolling mean of coordinates, exponential weighted mean, speed percentile
Interaction features: relative distance, lead angles, chase metrics, speed correlation for mouse pairs
Single mouse features: pairwise squared body part distances, lagged ear/tail speeds, elongation, body angle, rolling coordinate stats, nose-tail distance lags

Pair features: squared distances between all body part combinations, relative orientation, nose approach, distance bins, rolling distance stats, interaction correlation, nose distance lags/close probability
ensembling: Optimizes CatBoost, XGBoost, and XGBoost-2 weights per action class using Optuna on OOF predictions, applies per-action probability thresholds, and post-processes predictions by thresholding, handling frame transitions, extending to video end, and removing overlapping predictions.
insights: Raw 2D pose coordinates can be transformed into rich motion features by scaling temporal windows to physical time and computing derivatives, rolling statistics, and relative kinematics.
Per-action gradient boosting models can be effectively combined using Optuna-optimized weights and thresholds rather than fixed averaging or equal weighting.
Careful post-processing (handling sparse labels, overlapping frames, and empty videos) is critical for valid submission formats in frame-level action recognition.
